questions.question:
Statements:
All cats are animals.
Some animals are pets.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Cats → animals, but no link to pets → I doesn't follow. All pets being animals is valid.

questions.question:
Statements:
All pencils are tools.
No tool is a toy.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Pencils ⊆ tools and tools ≠ toys → I follows. Since no overlap, some toys are definitely not pencils → II follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some books are novels.
All novels are stories.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some books → novels → stories → I follows. Cannot extend to all books → II false.

questions.question:
Statements:
No dog is a cat.
All cats are mammals.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Dogs may still be mammals → I doesn't follow. Some mammals are cats is valid → II follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
All roses are flowers.
Some flowers are red.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: No direct link roses ↔ red → I doesn't follow. Some flowers are red → II follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some teachers are writers.
All writers are creative.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some teachers → writers → creative → I follows. Cannot generalize to all teachers.

questions.question:
Statements:
All apples are fruits.
No fruit is a vegetable.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Apples ⊆ fruits, fruits ≠ vegetables → I follows. Hence vegetables ≠ apples → II follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
All engineers are graduates.
Some graduates are unemployed.
No unemployed person is rich.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some graduates → unemployed → not rich → II follows. No guarantee engineers are in that subset → I doesn't follow.

questions.question:
Statements:
No artist is a scientist.
Some scientists are researchers.
All researchers are educated.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
conclusions: Some educated people are not artists
questions.explanation: Scientists ≠ artists → II follows. Scientists → researchers → educated → hence some educated ≠ artists → I follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
All fruits are healthy.
Some healthy items are expensive.
No expensive item is local.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
conclusions: All local items are not fruits
questions.explanation: No direct fruits ↔ expensive link → I uncertain. II is reverse assumption → invalid.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some cars are electric.
All electric vehicles are silent.
No silent vehicle is cheap.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some cars → electric → silent → not cheap → I follows. Cannot extend to all cars.

questions.question:
Statements:
All A are B.
No B is C.
Some C are D.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: A ⊆ B and B ≠ C → I follows. Some C → D and C ≠ B → II follows. Since A ⊆ B, D linked to C cannot be A → III follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some players are singers.
No singer is a dancer.
All dancers are fit.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
conclusions: No fit person is a singer
questions.explanation: Some players → singers and singers ≠ dancers → I follows. Fit ↔ singer relation not fixed → II false.

questions.question:
Statements:
All metals are solids.
Some solids are liquids.
No liquid is gas.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Metals ⊆ solids but solids overlap liquids doesn't ensure metals do → I is possibility but not definite. Metals ⊆ solids and liquids ≠ gas → metals ≠ gas → II follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some A are B.
All B are C.
Some C are not D.
No D is A.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: A ∩ B exists and D ≠ A → I follows. B ⊆ C and some C not D → some B may not be D → II follows. No guarantee C overlaps A fully → III uncertain.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some chairs are tables.
All tables are wooden.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some chairs → tables → wooden → I follows. Reverse (wooden → tables) invalid → II false.

questions.question:
Statements:
No bird is a mammal.
Some mammals are aquatic.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
conclusions: Some aquatic animals are not birds
No aquatic animal is a bird
questions.explanation: Some mammals → aquatic, mammals ≠ birds → I follows. II overgeneralizes.

questions.question:
Statements:
All pens are stationery.
Some stationery are expensive.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: No direct link pens ↔ expensive → I false. Some stationery → expensive → II true.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some mountains are hills.
All hills are green.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some mountains → hills → green → I follows. Reverse fails.

questions.question:
Statements:
No laptop is a phone.
All phones are electronic.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Phones ⊆ electronics and phones ≠ laptops → some electronics ≠ laptops → I true. II unsupported.

questions.question:
Statements:
All actors are famous.
Some famous people are rich.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: No guarantee actors overlap rich → I false. Some rich ↔ famous → II true.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some rivers are lakes.
No lake is salty.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some rivers → lakes → not salty → I follows. Cannot extend to all rivers.

questions.question:
Statements:
All poets are thinkers.
Some thinkers are introverts.
No introvert is social.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Some thinkers → introverts → not social → II follows. No guarantee poets fall in that subset → I false. III false.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some A are B.
No B is C.
All C are D.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: A ∩ B exists and B ≠ C → I follows. C ⊆ D and C ≠ B → some D ≠ B → II follows. No A--D link → III false.

questions.question:
Statements:
All smartphones are gadgets.
Some gadgets are fragile.
Some fragile items are costly.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Smartphones → gadgets; gadgets ↔ fragile ↔ costly chain allows possibility → I valid (may). II definite via fragile. III reverse false.

questions.question:
Statements:
No teacher is lazy.
Some lazy people are unhealthy.
All unhealthy people are stressed.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
conclusions: Some stressed people are not teachers
No stressed person is a teacher
questions.explanation: Lazy → unhealthy → stressed, lazy ≠ teacher → some stressed ≠ teachers → I follows. II too strong. III no link.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some engineers are managers.
All managers are experienced.
No experienced person is a fresher.
Some freshers are trainees.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
statements: No experienced person is a fresher
questions.explanation: Engineers → managers → experienced → not fresher → I follows. Freshers ≠ experienced → trainees (subset) may include → II follows. III no link.

questions.question:
Statements:
All squares are rectangles.
Some rectangles are circles.
No circle is a triangle.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: Rectangles overlap circles → squares (subset) may → I follows (possibility). Circles ≠ triangles → rectangles linked → II follows. Squares ⊆ rectangles → not triangles → III follows.

questions.question:
Statements:
Some books are journals.
All journals are indexed.
Some indexed items are not digital.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
statements: Some indexed items are not digital
conclusions: Some books may not be digital
questions.explanation: Books → journals → indexed; indexed ↔ not digital → books may be → I valid. II not guaranteed. III overgeneralization.

questions.question:
Statements:
No A is B.
Some B are C.
All C are D.
Some D are E.

Which conclusion(s) follow?
questions.explanation: B ≠ A and C overlaps B → some C ≠ A → I follows. C ⊆ D → some D ≠ A → II follows. D overlaps E and D comes from C/B chain → some E ≠ B → III follows.

questions.question:

If the ground is wet, the match is canceled.
questions.explanation: Rain → wet ground → match canceled.

questions.scenario: A number is even and greater than 2.
questions.explanation: Even (>2) → divisible by 2 → not prime.

questions.question:

questions.question:

If they miss the train, they take a taxi.
If they take a taxi, they spend extra money.
If they are not late, they catch the train.
questions.scenario: A person does not spend extra money.
questions.explanation: No extra spending → no taxi → no missed train → not late → catch train.

questions.question:
Rules:
If A occurs, B occurs.
If B occurs, C occurs.
If C occurs, D does not occur.
D occurs only if E occurs.
If E does not occur, F occurs.
Scenario: A occurs.
Which MUST be true?
rules: If A occurs, B occurs.
If B occurs, C occurs.
If C occurs, D does not occur.
D occurs only if E occurs.
If E does not occur, F occurs.
questions.explanation: A → B → C → not D. Since D needs E, and D is false → E must be false → F occurs.
